channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: North Las Vegas police officer dies after shooting
item.description: A North Las Vegas police officer shot multiple times Tuesday afternoon has died from his injuries, several sources tell 8 News Now.
item.dc:creator: Stephanie Overton, Fox 5

item.title: Protests against Trump and Project 2025 are planned in cities across the US
item.description: A movement to oppose the early actions of President Donald Trump’s administration is taking off online, with plans to protest across the U.S. on Wednesday. The movement has organized under the hashtags #buildtheresistance and #50501, which stands for 50 protests, 50 states, one day.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Baltimore mayor fights back against attacks on DEI initiatives with ‘Definitely Earned It’ campaign
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump's attack on diversity, equity and inclusion programs have led military and government agencies — along with some private companies — to roll back observances of diversity and celebrations of Black History Month, which goes through February.
item.dc:creator: MATT BROWN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump administration will consider redrawing boundaries of national monuments as part of energy push
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — As part of the Trump administration’s push to expand U.S. energy production, federal officials will review and consider redrawing the boundaries of national monuments created under previous presidents to protect unique landscapes and cultural resources.
item.dc:creator: MATTHEW BROWN and MATTHEW DALY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Live updates: Trump suggests Gaza should 'not be rebuilt' in joint presser with Netanyahu
item.description: President Trump and Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu are holding a joint press conference at the White House on Tuesday where they are discussing the future of the Gaza Strip.
item.dc:creator: The Hill staff, WGN 9

item.title: US military flying surveillance aircraft for reconnaissance missions along southern border with Mexico
item.description: The U.S. military is conducting ISR missions via aircraft along the southern border under President Donald Trump's executive order addressing border security and immigration.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump wants rare earth elements from Ukraine in exchange for military aid
item.description: “We're looking to do a deal with Ukraine, where they're going to secure what we're giving them with their rare earth and other things,” he told reporters.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Frazin, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: China hits US with retaliatory tariffs
item.description: China hit the U.S. back with retaliatory tariffs across the board over President Trump’s enforcement of tariffs on Chinese goods that went into effect on Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, KRON4

item.title: Philadelphia boy praised as hero for shielding sister during plane crash
item.description: A grade schooler is being credited for saving his sister from falling debris during the northeast Philadelphia plane crash on Jan. 31, 2025 that left seven people dead.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: El Salvador offers to accept deportees from US of any nationality, including Americans: Rubio
item.description: The U.S. government cannot deport American citizens and such a move would be met with significant legal challenges.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: China responds after Trump's tariffs take effect and more top headlines
item.description: Get all the stories you need-to-know from the most powerful name in news delivered first thing every morning to your inbox.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Schools poised to be major front line in Trump immigration crackdown
item.description: Oklahoma is moving to require proof of citizenship for the guardians of public school students, while California schools are distributing "know your rights" cards to immigrant families as K-12 campuses become a crucial front line in President Trump's immigration crackdown.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, Fox 5

item.title: Major League Baseball fires ump amid gambling probe
item.description: Umpire Pat Hoberg was fired after it was revealed he shared betting accounts with a friend who bet on baseball.
item.dc:creator: Travis Schlepp, KRON4

item.title: Beloved park volunteer found dead after hike in Great Smoky Mountains
item.description: A hiker, who had been reported as missing, was found dead in the Great Smoky Mountains National Park on Sunday.
item.dc:creator: Matt Hollingsworth, KRON4

item.title: Some US businesses close in a 'day without immigrants.' But many say they can't lose income
item.description: Several businesses from day cares to grocery stores and hair salons closed Monday across the U.S. in a loosely organized day of protest against President Donald Trump's immigration policies.
item.dc:creator: GIOVANNA DELL'ORTO and MELISSA PEREZ WINDER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Salvage crews remove large portion of commercial jet from river after deadly air collision near DC
item.description: Salvage crews have removed a large portion of a commercial jet from the Potomac River near Washington’s Reagan National Airport on Monday, five days after a midair collision last week that killed 67 people.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Democrat places blanket hold on Trump’s State Department picks over targeting of USAID
item.description: Sen. Brian Schatz (D-Hawaii), a member of the Senate Foreign Relations Committee, announced Monday he will place a blanket hold on all of President Trump’s nominees to the State Department until the president backs off his effort to shut down USAID, the nation’s chief foreign assistance agency.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Dozens of Education Department staffers on leave amid Trump's DEI purge
item.description: Dozens of employees with the Department of Education were placed on administrative leave on Friday, allegedly after attending a diversity training course encouraged by then-Secretary Betsy DeVos during the first Trump administration.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, WGN 9

item.title: Trump, Mexican president agree to delay tariffs for one month
item.description: President Trump and Mexican President Claudia Sheinbaum said they have agreed to delay tariffs by at least one month, following a call between the two leaders Monday. The 25 percent tariffs on Mexico were to be implemented Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Minneapolis to pay woman who says Chauvin knelt on her back. Here's how much
item.description: The city of Minneapolis will pay $600,000 to a woman who alleged that former policeman Derek Chauvin pinned her to the ground with his knee in January 2020, just months before he used the same maneuver on George Floyd.
item.dc:creator: Cheyanne M. Daniels, Fox 5

item.title: Trump kept his tariffs pledge. Is he ready for the fallout? Are Americans? What to know
item.description: The Budget Lab at Yale University estimates Trump’s tariffs would cost the average American household $1,000 to $1,200 in annual purchasing power.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Army identifies 3rd soldier killed in DC crash
item.description: The U.S. Army on Saturday identified the third soldier who died last week during a training mission that ended in a midair crash between an Army Black Hawk helicopter and an American Airlines regional jet near Ronald Reagan Washington National Airport.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, WGN 9

item.title: Officials say remains of 55 of 67 victims of midair collision have been recovered and identified
item.description: Families of victims of the deadliest U.S. air disaster since 2001 visited the crash site on Sunday and divers scoured the submerged wreckage for more remains after authorities said they've recovered and identified 55 of the 67 people killed.
item.dc:creator: DAVID R. MARTIN, MICHAEL R. SISAK and CLAUDIA LAUER Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Mayor says 5 injured in fatal Philadelphia plane crash remain hospitalized, 3 in critical condition
item.description: Three people injured on the ground when a jet crashed in a busy Philadelphia neighborhood, killing seven people, remain in critical condition, Mayor Cherelle Parker said Sunday. Parker said 22 people were injured and five of them remain hospitalized.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Black box recovered in Philadelphia medical transport jet crash: NTSB
item.description: The cockpit voice recorder was located at the site of initial impact, at a depth of 8 feet, according to the NTSB.
item.dc:creator: Mills Hayes, WGN 9

item.title: Jimmy Carter wins posthumous Grammy for narrating an audiobook of his Sunday school lessons
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Former President Jimmy Carter has won a posthumous Grammy award. Carter, the peanut farmer who won the presidency in the wake of the Watergate scandal and Vietnam War, died in December at age 100.
item.dc:creator: MARIA SHERMAN, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Luka Doncic heads to the Lakers and Anthony Davis goes to the Mavs in blockbuster trade
item.description: The trade was put together in secret, with only a precious few people knowing what was happening. Luka Doncic didn't know. Anthony Davis didn't know. Their coaches didn't know. Their teammates didn't know. And then it happened — an absolute blockbuster.
item.dc:creator: TIM REYNOLDS, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Passengers, crew killed in 'high-impact' air ambulance crash in Philadelphia identified
item.description: Authorities said a seventh person, who had been in a car at the time of the crash, has died. Their identity has not yet been released.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, ABC 4

item.title: South Carolina retires jersey number of three-time WNBA MVP A’ja Wilson
item.description: Three-time WNBA MVP A’ja Wilson's South Carolina No. 22 jersey was hung in the rafters Sunday at Colonial Life Arena, her college home court that already features a statue of the program’s all-time leading scorer outside the main entrance.
item.dc:creator: PETE IACOBELLI, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: How to save money on your taxes
item.description: You may qualify for tax credits for caring for a child or dependent or investing in clean energy for your home.
item.dc:creator: Steph Whiteside, Fox 5

item.title: Ohtani's return to pitching could come in May for World Series champion Dodgers
item.description:
Shohei Ohtani pitching for the Los Angeles Dodgers in May is "about right,” manager Dave Roberts said Saturday at the team's annual fan fest.
He reiterated that Ohtani won't pitch in the season-opening Japan Series against the Chicago Cubs on March 18-19 in Tokyo.
item.dc:creator: BETH HARRIS, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Will La Niña be a 'bust' this winter? Meteorologist explains why it may feel like it
item.description: After months of teasing, a La Niña winter has finally started – but we don't blame you if you haven't noticed.
item.dc:creator: Alix Martichoux, Fox 5
